hyperedges.label: Tenant Isolation Defense-in-Depth
hyperedges.description: Five mutually-reinforcing mechanisms that together prevent cross-tenant data leakage: ThreadLocal context filter, shared-schema composite indexes, RBAC @PreAuthorize, IDOR threat model (T-002), and automated cross-tenant test matrix; ADR-002 documents the trade-off accepted

hyperedges.label: MVP Cost/Complexity Trade-off Cluster
hyperedges.description: Four deliberate MVP simplifications: Caffeine (no Redis), Docker Compose (no Kubernetes), modular monolith (no microservices), outbox polling (no RabbitMQ/Kafka); each has documented replacement triggers for post-MVP scaling

hyperedges.label: KVKK Compliance Architecture
hyperedges.description: Six components forming the KVKK (Turkish GDPR) compliance stack: data inventory with legal basis, granular versioned consent, AOP audit trail, AES-256-GCM field encryption, 72h breach notification procedure, and operational runbook for tenant data leak incidents

nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: Saha Flow SaaS Platform
nodes.description: Turkish B2B SaaS for field service management (HVAC, security, maintenance), targeting 5-50 technician firms

nodes.label: MVP Scope
nodes.description: Tenant/customer/device mgmt, work orders, Flutter mobile offline-first, PDF reports, payment tracking, basic reporting. Excludes: stock, live GPS, customer portal, AI routing, payment integration, white-label

nodes.label: Target Market (5-50 tech firms, Turkey then MENA)
nodes.description: Small Turkish technical service firms replacing paper/WhatsApp/Excel; expansion to MENA and Eastern Europe post-MVP
nodes.norm_label: target market (5-50 tech firms, turkey then mena)

nodes.label: JWT Auth Assumption V-011 (access 15min, refresh 7day)
nodes.description: Validated assumption: access token 15min, refresh 7-day with rotation. Brute-force risk mitigated by account lock at 5 failures

nodes.label: Offline Mobile Constraint V-010 (50 WO, 200 photos max)
nodes.description: Max 50 work orders and 200 photos cached offline per technician; SQLite storage ~100MB
nodes.norm_label: offline mobile constraint v-010 (50 wo, 200 photos max)

nodes.label: Shared Schema Multi-tenancy Assumption V-024
nodes.description: Single PostgreSQL DB, single schema, tenant_id on every table; re-evaluate at 100+ tenants

nodes.label: Work Order Lifecycle (BEKLIYOR→TAMAMLANDI/IPTAL)
nodes.description: Full lifecycle: created by dispatcher, assigned to technician, technician travels/starts/completes, customer signs, PDF generated

nodes.label: User Personas (Ahmet owner / Ayşe dispatcher / Mehmet technician)
nodes.description: Three primary personas driving product decisions: firm owner (billing/reports), office staff (dispatch), field technician (mobile)
nodes.norm_label: user personas (ahmet owner / ayse dispatcher / mehmet technician)

nodes.label: Pricing Model (500/450/400 TL per technician/month)
nodes.description: Tiered pricing: 500 TL (1-10 techs), 450 TL (11-30), 400 TL (31-50+). Target 250K TL MRR in 6 months

nodes.label: Acceptance Criteria AK-01..AK-07
nodes.description: Key criteria: 2min customer+WO creation, 30sec check-in, 4hr offline operation, 10sec PDF generation

nodes.label: 12 Bounded Contexts (DDD)
nodes.description: Identity, Tenant, Customer, Asset, WorkOrder, Scheduling, Checklist, Inventory, Files, Notification, Audit, Shared

nodes.label: WorkOrder Entity (core aggregate with @Version optimistic lock)
nodes.description: Central aggregate: CheckIn, CheckOut, Photo, Signature, PaymentRecord sub-entities; optimistic locking via @Version
nodes.norm_label: workorder entity (core aggregate with @version optimistic lock)

nodes.label: WorkOrder State Machine (BEKLIYOR→ATANDI→YOLDA→BASLADI→TAMAMLANDI/IPTAL)
nodes.description: Role-permitted state transitions; 15 business invariants (BR-001..BR-015); optimistic lock on concurrent updates

nodes.label: Domain Events (10 events, sync/async)
nodes.description: WORK_ORDER_CREATED, ASSIGNED, CHECKED_IN, COMPLETED etc; async events published via outbox pattern

nodes.label: BFF Pattern (Next.js proxies all API calls via /api/proxy)
nodes.rationale: Single entry point; JWT never exposed to browser JavaScript; enables server-side rendering with API auth
nodes.norm_label: bff pattern (next.js proxies all api calls via /api/proxy)

nodes.label: Transactional Outbox Pattern (outbox_event PostgreSQL table + polling publisher)
nodes.rationale: Avoids dual-write problem; no RabbitMQ/Kafka for MVP; at-least-once delivery via idempotent polling; 1-5s processing delay accepted
nodes.norm_label: transactional outbox pattern (outbox_event postgresql table + polling publisher)

nodes.label: S3 Presigned URL Upload/Download (15min TTL, never through backend)
nodes.norm_label: s3 presigned url upload/download (15min ttl, never through backend)

nodes.label: Spring Boot 3.3 + Java 21 Virtual Threads (modular monolith)
nodes.description: 12 domain modules in single JVM; Project Loom virtual threads for I/O concurrency; package boundary enforced by ArchUnit
nodes.norm_label: spring boot 3.3 + java 21 virtual threads (modular monolith)

nodes.label: Flutter 3.22 Offline-First (Hive/Isar local storage, sync engine)
nodes.description: UI reads always from local DB; changes queued and synced on reconnect; SQLCipher AES-256 encryption; last-write-wins for MVP conflicts
nodes.norm_label: flutter 3.22 offline-first (hive/isar local storage, sync engine)

nodes.label: Caffeine In-Memory Cache (Redis deferred post-MVP)
nodes.rationale: MVP cost savings; same JVM avoids network hop; replacement trigger: distributed deployment or >50 tenants with cache pressure

nodes.label: Docker Compose + Hetzner VPS (Kubernetes deferred)
nodes.rationale: 2-person team, MVP speed; single-server sufficient; Kubernetes re-evaluation at 50+ tenants or HA requirement

nodes.label: HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict Cookie (JWT transport)
nodes.description: Browser never sees JWT token; access token cookie 15min, refresh token cookie path-restricted to /api/auth/refresh

nodes.label: Content Security Policy Headers (middleware.ts, frame-ancestors none)
nodes.description: CSP blocks inline scripts; X-Frame-Options DENY; CSRF via SameSite=Strict + Spring CSRF token
nodes.norm_label: content security policy headers (middleware.ts, frame-ancestors none)

nodes.label: Zod Schema Client-Side Validation (workOrderCreateSchema example)
nodes.description: Client-side validation with Zod; server-side validation always authoritative; react-hook-form integration

nodes.label: TenantContextFilter + TenantEntityInterceptor (ThreadLocal isolation)
nodes.description: Extracts tenant_id from JWT, writes to ThreadLocal; Hibernate interceptor auto-sets on save, validates on delete; repository always findByIdAndTenantId

nodes.label: RBAC (ADMIN/MANAGER/DISPATCHER/TECHNICIAN, @PreAuthorize method-level)
nodes.description: 4 roles; @PreAuthorize with hasAuthority() + @tenantValidator.isSameTenant(); ArchUnit enforces annotation presence on all controllers

nodes.label: JWT Authentication (access 15min, refresh 7day, rotation + reuse detection)
nodes.description: Claims: sub, tenant_id, permissions[], role, jti; refresh rotation invalidates all sessions on reuse detection; BCrypt cost=12 passwords
nodes.norm_label: jwt authentication (access 15min, refresh 7day, rotation + reuse detection)

nodes.label: Rate Limiting (Bucket4j + Caffeine, login 5/min, files 10/min, general 60/min)
nodes.norm_label: rate limiting (bucket4j + caffeine, login 5/min, files 10/min, general 60/min)

nodes.label: Audit Log (@Auditable AOP aspect + pgAudit SQL-level + audit_event table)
nodes.description: actor_id, timestamp, old_status, new_status, ip_address per event; partitioned by month; 2-year retention for KVKK
nodes.norm_label: audit log (@auditable aop aspect + pgaudit sql-level + audit_event table)

nodes.label: AES-256-GCM Field Encryption (JPA AttributeConverter, SecureRandom IV)
nodes.description: Sensitive fields encrypted at application layer; base64 encoded IV+ciphertext; EncryptedStringConverter on entity fields
nodes.norm_label: aes-256-gcm field encryption (jpa attributeconverter, securerandom iv)

nodes.label: Shared DB / Shared Schema (tenant_id NOT NULL on all tables, composite index)
nodes.rationale: Single DB/backup/migration for 2-person team; IDOR prevention via composite (tenant_id, id) indexes; noisy-neighbor risk accepted for MVP
nodes.norm_label: shared db / shared schema (tenant_id not null on all tables, composite index)

nodes.label: PostgreSQL 16 + PostGIS (ST_DWithin, ST_Distance geospatial queries)
nodes.description: GiST indexes for geometry; customer_address stores PostGIS Point; location_event partitioned by month (~100GB largest table)

nodes.label: Flyway Versioned Migrations V001..V012 + repeatable functions
nodes.description: app_migration role for DDL, app_runtime for DML only; migration-before-deploy ordering; staging validation required before production
nodes.norm_label: flyway versioned migrations v001..v012 + repeatable functions

nodes.label: Backup Strategy (pg_dump + WAL PITR, RPO 1h, RTO 4h, 30-day retention)
nodes.description: Weekly full + WAL archiving; GPG encrypted backups; cross-VPS rsync; KVKK deletion within 90 days from backups
nodes.norm_label: backup strategy (pg_dump + wal pitr, rpo 1h, rto 4h, 30-day retention)

nodes.label: RESTful API (45+ endpoints, OpenAPI 3.0, Richardson Maturity L2)
nodes.description: URL: /api/v{n}/tenants/{tenantId}/{resource}; openapi-generator for TypeScript/Dart clients; pagination offset-based max 100; sort whitelist enforced
nodes.norm_label: restful api (45+ endpoints, openapi 3.0, richardson maturity l2)

nodes.label: Webhook (HMAC-SHA256 signing, 5min replay protection, exponential retry)
nodes.description: X-SahaFlow-Signature header; timestamp+nonce replay prevention; 5 attempts exponential backoff; outbound only
nodes.norm_label: webhook (hmac-sha256 signing, 5min replay protection, exponential retry)

nodes.label: STRIDE Threat Analysis (26 threats T-001..T-026, risk matrix)
nodes.description: Risk levels: Kritik (T-002 IDOR, T-007 file upload, T-018 supply chain), Yuksek (T-001 credential stuffing, T-010 JWT hijack)
nodes.norm_label: stride threat analysis (26 threats t-001..t-026, risk matrix)

nodes.label: IDOR / Tenant Bypass Threat T-002 (Critical, impact 5, probability 3)
nodes.description: Attacker modifies tenant_id in header/body to access other tenant data; mitigated by JWT-vs-URL tenant_id comparison + WHERE tenant_id = :tid
nodes.norm_label: idor / tenant bypass threat t-002 (critical, impact 5, probability 3)

nodes.label: Supply Chain Attack T-018 (Critical, malicious NPM/Maven package)
nodes.description: Malicious dependency injected via npm/Maven; mitigated by Dependabot + Trivy SCA + lockfile pinning + SBOM CycloneDX
nodes.norm_label: supply chain attack t-018 (critical, malicious npm/maven package)

nodes.label: KVKK Data Inventory (PII categories, legal basis per processing activity)
nodes.description: Categories: Kimlik, Iletisim, Adres, Konum, Gorsel, Cihaz, IslemGuvenligi, Finans; legal basis mapped for each; 10-year retention for financial data
nodes.norm_label: kvkk data inventory (pii categories, legal basis per processing activity)

nodes.label: Consent Management (versioned, revocable, granular per processing type)
nodes.description: consent_id, user_id, consent_type, granted, timestamp, ip_address, consent_version stored in DB; GPS tracking requires explicit opt-in
nodes.norm_label: consent management (versioned, revocable, granular per processing type)

nodes.label: Data Breach Response (72h KVKK Board notification obligation)
nodes.description: Containment within 4h, tenant notification within 12h, KVKK Board within 72h, RCA within 5 business days
nodes.norm_label: data breach response (72h kvkk board notification obligation)

nodes.label: GitHub Actions CI/CD (ci-web.yml, ci-api.yml, ci-mobile.yml, deploy-staging.yml)
nodes.description: Monorepo, path-filtered triggers; quality gate: lint→unit test→SAST→SCA→integration test→container scan→SBOM→build; staging auto-deploy, prod manual approval
nodes.norm_label: github actions ci/cd (ci-web.yml, ci-api.yml, ci-mobile.yml, deploy-staging.yml)

nodes.label: Supply Chain Security (SBOM CycloneDX, Trivy, Gitleaks, Dependabot, SLSA L2 target)
nodes.description: Lockfile pinning; Hadolint for Dockerfiles; image signed with Cosign (target); provenance attestation; OWASP Dependency Check for Java
nodes.norm_label: supply chain security (sbom cyclonedx, trivy, gitleaks, dependabot, slsa l2 target)

nodes.label: Test Pyramid (300+ unit, 60-80 integration Testcontainers, 5-10 E2E Playwright)
nodes.description: 70% unit / 20% integration / 10% E2E+contract; @DataJpaTest + Testcontainers PostGIS; Pact consumer-driven contract; k6 load tests
nodes.norm_label: test pyramid (300+ unit, 60-80 integration testcontainers, 5-10 e2e playwright)

nodes.label: TenantIsolationTest (cross-tenant access matrix, 6 entity types)
nodes.description: TenantIsolationTest.java: WorkOrder/Customer/File/Invoice/Report/UserList all return 404/403 when accessed cross-tenant; also tests header manipulation

nodes.label: Negative Test Suite NT-01..NT-25 (JWT, IDOR, file upload, rate limit, optimistic lock)
nodes.description: 25 mandatory negative tests covering expired JWT, cross-tenant access, 100MB upload, .php file, ZIP bomb, EICAR, rate limit breach, HMAC webhook
nodes.norm_label: negative test suite nt-01..nt-25 (jwt, idor, file upload, rate limit, optimistic lock)

nodes.label: OpenTelemetry Stack (OTel Collector → Prometheus + Loki + Tempo + Grafana)
nodes.rationale: Vendor-neutral; self-hosted for KVKK compliance (all telemetry stays in Turkey); 10% trace sampling in production; 30-day Prometheus retention
nodes.norm_label: opentelemetry stack (otel collector → prometheus + loki + tempo + grafana)

nodes.label: SLO Definitions (login 99.9%, API p95 <500ms, mobile sync 99%, file upload 99.5%)
nodes.description: Error budget: login ~43min/month, file upload ~3.6h/month; 15 Grafana alarms with PagerDuty for P0
nodes.norm_label: slo definitions (login 99.9%, api p95 <500ms, mobile sync 99%, file upload 99.5%)

nodes.label: ADR-001: Modular Monolith over Microservices (Accepted)
nodes.rationale: 2-person team; single transaction = data + outbox event consistency; strangler-fig extraction path; re-evaluate at 100+ tenants or team 5+

nodes.label: ADR-002: Shared DB/Shared Schema Multi-tenancy (Accepted)
nodes.rationale: Single backup/migration/connection pool; noisy-neighbor risk accepted; re-evaluate at 100 tenants or any tenant hitting 100GB

nodes.label: ADR-003: Next.js BFF + HttpOnly Cookie Auth (Accepted)
nodes.rationale: XSS cannot steal JWT; SSR/ISR possible; API URL hidden from client; CSRF mitigated by SameSite=Strict + Spring CSRF token
nodes.norm_label: adr-003: next.js bff + httponly cookie auth (accepted)

nodes.label: ADR-004: Flutter Offline-First Architecture (Accepted)
nodes.rationale: Unreliable field connectivity is a first-class constraint; last-write-wins for MVP; CRDT considered for V1 if conflicts frequent

nodes.label: ADR-008: Transactional Outbox Pattern (no RabbitMQ/Kafka) (Accepted)
nodes.rationale: Atomic event + data write; no broker infrastructure; polling delay 1-5s acceptable; re-evaluate at 1000+ events/min

nodes.label: Project Phases (Discovery W1-W2 → Foundation W3-W5 → MVP W6-W13 → Pilot W14-W17 → V1 W21+)
nodes.description: Total 21 weeks to production; 2-person team; 158 story points MVP Must-Have; pilot with 1-2 real firms at W14
nodes.norm_label: project phases (discovery w1-w2 → foundation w3-w5 → mvp w6-w13 → pilot w14-w17 → v1 w21+)

nodes.label: MVP Epics (AUTH/CUSTOMER/WORKORDER/ASSIGN/MOBILE/CHECKLIST/REPORT/DASHBOARD)
nodes.description: 8 epics with must/should/could prioritization; V1 features: route optimization, stock, customer portal, SSO, white-label

nodes.label: Definition of Done (9 categories: Product/Code/Test/Security/Data/Docs/Observability/Ops/Release)
nodes.description: 80+ criteria; 80% unit test coverage; OWASP ASVS L2 for security; every endpoint needs @PreAuthorize; tenant isolation test mandatory per entity

nodes.label: Operations Runbook (13 scenarios: login failure, DB slowness, deploy rollback, backup restore)
nodes.description: Each runbook: symptoms, 5-min triage, PromQL/LogQL queries, mitigation, permanent fix, rollback condition, escalation chain; RTO 4h, RPO 1h
nodes.norm_label: operations runbook (13 scenarios: login failure, db slowness, deploy rollback, backup restore)

nodes.label: Runbook: Tenant Data Leak Response (Runbooks 2+3, KVKK 72h obligation)
nodes.description: Immediate session revocation, audit log cross-tenant query, RLS activation option, tenant notification, KVKK Board notification within 72h
nodes.norm_label: runbook: tenant data leak response (runbooks 2+3, kvkk 72h obligation)

nodes.label: Shared DB Shared Schema Tenant Model
nodes.norm_label: shared db shared schema tenant model

nodes.label: Security Scan Job (Gitleaks, Trivy, npm audit, OWASP)
nodes.norm_label: security scan job (gitleaks, trivy, npm audit, owasp)

nodes.label: Build and Scan Docker Images Job
nodes.norm_label: build and scan docker images job

nodes.label: Integration Test Job (Full Stack Smoke Test)
nodes.norm_label: integration test job (full stack smoke test)

nodes.label: Secure SDLC Requirement (NFR-SEC-03, OWASP ASVS L2)
nodes.norm_label: secure sdlc requirement (nfr-sec-03, owasp asvs l2)

hyperedges.label: Tenant Isolation Defense-in-Depth
hyperedges.description: Five mutually-reinforcing mechanisms that together prevent cross-tenant data leakage: ThreadLocal context filter, shared-schema composite indexes, RBAC @PreAuthorize, IDOR threat model (T-002), and automated cross-tenant test matrix; ADR-002 documents the trade-off accepted

hyperedges.label: MVP Cost/Complexity Trade-off Cluster
hyperedges.description: Four deliberate MVP simplifications: Caffeine (no Redis), Docker Compose (no Kubernetes), modular monolith (no microservices), outbox polling (no RabbitMQ/Kafka); each has documented replacement triggers for post-MVP scaling

hyperedges.label: KVKK Compliance Architecture
hyperedges.description: Six components forming the KVKK (Turkish GDPR) compliance stack: data inventory with legal basis, granular versioned consent, AOP audit trail, AES-256-GCM field encryption, 72h breach notification procedure, and operational runbook for tenant data leak incidents
